channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Whale birth takes a village — or, pod
item.description: Scientists got a rare look at the birth of a sperm whale and found that it took a group effort.
item.content:encoded: Scientists got a rare look at the birth of a sperm whale and found that it took a group effort.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Southeast Asia is being hit hard by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas
item.description: Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.
item.content:encoded:
Southeast Asia is among the areas hardest hit by Iran's cutoff of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, with many nations almost entirely dependent on foreign energy — and quickly running out.

(Image credit: Aaron Favila)
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: Comedian Julio Torres shares a moment of pride he experienced as a child
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Comedian Julio Torres talks about a moment of pride he experienced as a child.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Comedian Julio Torres talks about a moment of pride he experienced as a child.

item.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
item.description: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.
item.content:encoded: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: The government may soon lift restrictions on some peptide treatments
item.description: Peptide therapies for wellness and longevity are popular, but safety concerns led the FDA to place more stringent requirements on them. Now Health Secretary Kennedy says the rules will likely loosen.
item.content:encoded: Peptide therapies for wellness and longevity are popular, but safety concerns led the FDA to place more stringent requirements on them. Now Health Secretary Kennedy says the rules will likely loosen.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
item.description: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: The first major strike at a U.S. meatpacking plant in decades is in its second week
item.description: The first major strike at a U.S. meatpacking plant since 1985 is now in its second week in Greeley, Colo. The JBS beef plant there produces about 8% of the country's beef supply.
item.content:encoded: The first major strike at a U.S. meatpacking plant since 1985 is now in its second week in Greeley, Colo. The JBS beef plant there produces about 8% of the country's beef supply.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Mulholland

item.title: Higher oil prices are already affecting American businesses
item.description: Gasoline prices have jumped about a dollar a gallon since the war with Iran began. And diesel prices are up even more. That's tough on truckers, fishermen, and just about everyone else.
item.content:encoded: Gasoline prices have jumped about a dollar a gallon since the war with Iran began. And diesel prices are up even more. That's tough on truckers, fishermen, and just about everyone else.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Writer Rachel Knox wants people to re-think what they know about Florida
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with writer Rachel Knox about her new collection of essays, Anywhere Else.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with writer Rachel Knox about her new collection of essays, Anywhere Else.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Marquez Janse

item.title: AI affirms our own viewpoints and harms willingness to resolve conflict, study finds
item.description: The AI models and chatbots tend to validate our feelings and viewpoints — and provide advice accordingly. More so than people might, a new study finds — with potentially worrisome consequences.
item.content:encoded: The AI models and chatbots tend to validate our feelings and viewpoints — and provide advice accordingly. More so than people might, a new study finds — with potentially worrisome consequences.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
